_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-14 Protected Health Information (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-14: Protected Health Information — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: Govern the handling of protected health information (PHI) by Michigan EMS providers. EMS agencies are covered entities or business associates under HIPAA. Improper disclosure of PHI may result in civil and criminal penalties. Patient privacy must be protected at all times.

sections.title: What Constitutes PHI
points: PHI includes any individually identifiable health information in any form (written, electronic, verbal) that relates to a patient's past, present, or future health condition, treatment, or payment
Examples of PHI identifiers: name, address, date of birth, SSN, phone number, diagnosis, medications, PCR number, vehicle description, geographic data more specific than state
All ePCR and paper PCR records are PHI
Verbal communications about patients (radio, phone, face-to-face) are PHI and must be handled discreetly

sections.title: Permissible Disclosures
points: Treatment: PHI may be shared with other providers involved in the patient's treatment (receiving hospital, consulting physicians, transferring provider) without explicit patient authorization
Quality Improvement: MCA and PSRO access to PCRs for QI and QA is permissible
Legal: PHI may be disclosed pursuant to a lawful court order, subpoena, or law enforcement request per HIPAA requirements
Public Health: PHI may be disclosed to public health authorities for communicable disease reporting and surveillance as required by law
The minimum necessary standard applies — share only the PHI required for the specific permitted purpose

sections.title: Prohibited Disclosures
points: Social media: posting patient identifiable information on any social media platform is STRICTLY PROHIBITED — including patient photos, descriptions, or identifying information even without names
Media: sharing patient PHI with media or news outlets without patient authorization is PROHIBITED
Unauthorized personnel: sharing PHI with persons not involved in treatment is prohibited
Curiosity access: accessing PCRs of patients not in your care is prohibited
Public radio communications: minimize PHI on public radio frequencies; use HIPAA-compliant communication methods when possible

sections.title: Breach Reporting
points: Any potential PHI breach must be reported to the agency privacy officer or supervisor immediately
Breach notification to affected patients and DHHS may be required under HIPAA Breach Notification Rule
Agency must have a breach response plan including risk assessment, notification procedures, and corrective actions
Providers who knowingly and improperly disclose PHI are subject to disciplinary action, MCA sanction, and potential criminal prosecution

scope_notes: All PCR data (paper and electronic) is PHI
Permissible disclosures: treatment providers, QI/QA, legal order, public health
Minimum necessary standard applies to all disclosures
PROHIBITED: social media posting, media sharing, unauthorized personnel access
Radio: minimize PHI on public frequencies
Breach: report to privacy officer immediately
Social media PHI violations: subject to disciplinary action and criminal prosecution
